sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INTEGER, text_column TEXT, timestamp_column TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, text_column, timestamp_column) VALUES (1, 'test test', TIMESTAMP '1992-03-27 01:02:03');
INSERT INTO test_data (id, text_column, timestamp_column) VALUES (2, NULL, TIMESTAMP '1992-03-27 01:02:03');
INSERT INTO test_data (id, text_column, timestamp_column) VALUES (3, 'Hello, world!', TIMESTAMP '1992-03-27 01:02:03' - INTERVAL 2 DAY);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, text_column, timestamp_column) VALUES (4, '', TIMESTAMP '1992-03-27 01:02:03' - INTERVAL 30 DAY);
SELECT id, instr(text_column, 'es') AS position, timestamp_column - INTERVAL 3 DAY AS adjusted_time FROM test_data WHERE instr(text_column, 'test') > 0;
SELECT id, instr(text_column, 'o') AS position, timestamp_column - INTERVAL 1 DAY AS adjusted_time FROM test_data WHERE text_column IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, instr(text_column, 'test') AS position FROM test_data WHERE position IS NOT NULL GROUP BY id HAVING position > 0;
SELECT t.id, instr(t.text_column, 'Hello') / NULLIF(instr(t.text_column, 'o'), 0) AS ratio FROM test_data t WHERE t.text_column IS NOT NULL;
SELECT t1.id, t1.text_column, t2.timestamp_column FROM test_data t1 JOIN test_data t2 ON instr(t1.text_column, 'test') > 0 AND t1.id = t2.id;
SELECT id, text_column, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY instr(text_column, 't')) AS count FROM test_data WHERE instr(text_column, '') = 0;
